lenses.guidance: vv 1-31's encampment forms a square: Judah east, Reuben south, Ephraim west, Dan north. Three tribes per side, twelve in total, the tabernacle at center. The chapter's structure projects sacred geometry — God dwells in the middle.

vv 1-31's camp pattern — tabernacle at center, twelve tribes encircling — prefigures Rev 21:12-14's New Jerusalem with its twelve gates and twelve foundations. The pattern of God-in-the-midst spans Scripture from camp to city.

vv 3-31's twelve-tribe roster — east, south, west, north — echoes throughout Scripture as the canonical map. Ezek 48 reproduces the cardinal-direction layout for the eschatological land; Rev 21:12-13 lists the same four sides.
